sections.heading: What is Server-Side Caching?
sections.body: Redis, Memcached, in-memory caching, and cache invalidation. This lesson covers the essential concepts you need to understand to get started effectively.

sections.heading: Key Concepts
sections.body:
Server-Side Caching is a fundamental concept that every developer needs to master. Here are the core ideas:

1. Core Principle: The main idea behind Server-Side Caching is understanding how different components work together.

2. Application: In practice, you'll use Server-Side Caching every day when building applications. It's one of those skills that becomes second nature.

3. Common Patterns: Most Server-Side Caching implementations follow predictable patterns. Once you recognize them, you can apply them quickly.

sections.heading: Why This Matters
sections.body: Understanding Server-Side Caching is crucial because it builds the foundation for everything that follows. Master this topic and you'll find subsequent concepts much easier to learn.

summary: Server-Side Caching is a core concept in caching. Redis, Memcached, in-memory caching, and cache invalidation. Focus on understanding the fundamentals first, then practice applying them.
